entry.title: Washington Post: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Song Hae, a South Korean TV host with staying power, dies at 95
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Defenders hang on in Severodonetsk; Kyiv seeks corridor for grain exports
  • Car crashes into Berlin crowd, killing 1; driver is detained
  • The scene after a car crashed into a Berlin crowd, killing 1 and injuring several more
  • Inside the Taliban’s secret war in the Panjshir Valley
  • She was raped in Ukraine. How many others have stories like hers?
  • Merkel defends Ukraine policy, does not regret engaging Russia
  • Fiji court lets U.S. seize Russian oligarch’s $300 million superyacht
  • Severodonetsk defenders holding out under merciless shelling, mayor says
  • Lawyers for migrants say U.S. officials slowed family reunifications
  • Britain’s Johnson may be sinking at home, but he has fans in Ukraine
  • Dervla Murphy, intrepid author of travel books, dies at 90
  • Kyiv asks for more rocket systems as Kremlin warns of potential strikes in Russia
  • Cholera fears prompt quarantine in Mariupol, official says
  • Is Pope Francis nearing the end of his pontificate?
  • Hopes dim, anger grows in British journalist’s disappearance in Brazil
  • Dutch appeals court convicts Ethiopian of war 1970s crimes
  • Harvey Weinstein to be charged in UK over assault claims
  • Lagos ex-governor wins Nigerian ruling party's nomination
  • Talks to end Sudan crisis begin as anti-coup groups boycott
  • Train derailment in east Iran kills at least 21, injures 87
  • Iran says 2 UN watchdog devices at nuclear site turned off
  • Belgian king starts Congo visit, gives award to WWII veteran
  • Dutch police leaders vow to tackle racism in their ranks
  • Taliban detain Afghan fashion model on religious charges
  • Man sues American Airlines, says mistake led to 17-day jail ‘nightmare’
  • DNA ties ‘Pillowcase Rapist’ to six cold cases, officials say
  • 10-year-old in Fla. fatally shot woman arguing with her mom, police say
  • ‘Chrisley Knows Best’ reality TV stars convicted of fraud, tax evasion
  • Trump call Jan. 6 to ‘walk down to the Capitol’ prompted Secret Service scramble
  • Post Politics Now: Gun violence victims testifying in House; Biden to host Summit of the Americas
  • McConaughey urges gun measures in surprise White House appearance
  • Primary elections: San Francisco District Attorney Chesa Boudin is projected to be recalled amid anger over crime in the liberal city
  • Jan. 6 hearing will preempt ‘Young Sheldon’ — but not Tucker Carlson
  • The covid economy is harder for Black Americans
  • What is stagflation? Here’s why it matters and what you should know.
  • Crypto industry scores a big win under long-anticipated Senate bill
  • ‘The Quarry’ is a standout slasher that takes just a few wrong turns
  • Quick Fix: How to save your voicemails
  • Here’s how to preserve your important voice mails
  • Smartphones must have common charging port by 2024, E.U. says
  • Manassas Park embraces its youth, diversity and community spirit
  • Homeowner wishes to remove ex-spouse from mortgage without refinancing
  • Should you consider an adjustable-rate mortgage?
  • Climate Change Is a Military Problem for the US
  • Everyone Wants to Work for Big, Boring Companies Again
  • Plastic Recycling Is Working, So Ignore the Cynics
  • What Did We Learn From Tuesday’s Primaries?
  • Meet the Guptas, Symbols of South African Corruption
  • How a Battery Metals Squeeze Puts EV Future at Risk
  • Centrists’ new war on crime is also a war on the left
  • The federal budget has 99 problems. This program ain’t one — anymore.
  • When the pronoun police come for eighth-graders
  • Religion is not overpowering our politics
  • Let’s be honest: On guns, the enemy is us
  • Boudin’s recall proves Democrats have lost the public’s trust on crime
  • This Russian journalist blew up her life to protest Putin’s war. Now she’s in limbo.
  • Is there a sickness in U.S. culture? Yes: The GOP gun cult.
  • Why aren’t there more Republicans like Liz Cheney?
  • It’s true our Hawaiian Islands are overrun — and not just by chickens
  • Bob Casey: I changed my stance on guns. I shouldn’t be the only one.
  • The unexpected group of people thrilled with Boris Johnson's survival
  • A blunt lesson in a Montgomery graduation speech
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • What are the Democrats’ biggest problems? Chat with Perry Bacon Jr.
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 8 Jun, 2022

entry.title: Guardian: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Artist Paula Rego, known for her visceral and unsettling work, dies aged 87

  • The Spin | The many ages of Joe Root, the England great who always finds a way
  • Thailand to ease cannabis rules but smokers warned over smell ‘nuisance’
  • One person killed after car driven into crowd in Berlin
  • Lukaku joining Inter would suit all parties but it is easier said than done
  • ‘I never lost the joy!’: singer Gilbert O’Sullivan on love, loss and lawsuits
  • The best games of 2022 so far
  • ‘Gold rush’ for gas production threatens to lock in global heating
  • The split over Boris Johnson’s future is just the start of a massive Tory identity crisis | Rafael Behr
  • EasyJet diverted my flight and then refused to pay my taxi bill
  • A moment that changed me: I lost my home, moved into a van – and found freedom
  • ‘Biden blood only’: Hunter Biden’s ex-wife describes Secret Service exclusion

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 8 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-08 14:19PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • From cage homes to subdivided flats — what life is like in Hong Kong’s worst living spaces
  • Hong Kong police break up another money-laundering syndicate, arresting 16 linked to HK$2.5 billion in suspected crime proceeds
  • Hong Kong’s John Lee seeks legal exemptions for failing to comply with election rules during leadership bid
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong logs 558 new infections, even after officials tighten rules on caseloads
  • What is the MPF offsetting mechanism and why are workers happy the Hong Kong government is set to finally scrap it?
  • Fudan medical degree first from mainland China to be recognised in Hong Kong in special scheme, among 23 more overseas schools listed
  • Top advisers to Hong Kong leader defend decision to leave discussions on controversial hefty pay rise for civil servants to next administration
  • Woman arrested for outraging public decency after video of couple having sex on Hong Kong flat balcony posted online
  • Rescuers search for missing woman who fell into stream while hiking, as Hong Kong suspends afternoon classes amid rainstorm warning
  • Choosing French as a way to open doors
  • Hongkongers are more overworked and unhappy in their jobs than most of their Asia-Pacific peers, survey finds
  • Ocean zoning can help Hong Kong repair and rejuvenate its precious seas
  • Changes needed to Hong Kong public housing allocation policy to help cut waiting times for most needy, government advisers says
  • China’s Central Asia pledges made with eye on both US and Moscow, analysts say
  • China’s answer to open-source software giant Red Hat says its boss was taken away for disciplinary investigation
  • China’s transport restrictions have upended goods trade, Premier Li says in calling for logistical changes
  • What next for China’s zero-Covid policy after Shanghai lockdown?
  • Nike’s running app to stop service in China next month, following similar moves by Airbnb and Kindle
  • Taiwan expands intelligence operations centre to keep tabs on PLA
  • Shanghai reopening: Tesla’s Gigafactory 3 to exit ‘closed loop’ on June 11 as Covid-19 fears ease
  • China hails ‘ironclad partnership’ with Cambodia as work begins on naval base, raising US concern
  • ByteDance’s private valuation falls by US$100 billion in one year as IPO plans remain up in the air
  • China welcomes ‘important’ capital inflows with economy still reeling from coronavirus outbreaks
  • Huawei pursues more patent-licensing deals for 4G, 5G mobile technologies to boost sales amid struggles with US sanctions
  • China R&D key part of ‘global portfolio’, but technology leaks remain concern, report says
  • China gains ground on US in hi-tech ‘tug of war’, as Beijing spends billions on national champions
  • Taiwan on agenda at Shangri-La Dialogue as China, US defence chiefs to come face to face
  • Biden envoy heads to Marshall Islands in wake of China’s Pacific push
  • China offers rewards of over US$15,000 for national security tip-offs
  • Chinese university fires man who said women staff use children to avoid work and should give sex and salaries to male colleagues
  • Chinese academic warns there are legal risks to normalising Covid-19 testing
  • Deep dive for Chinese underwater lab in mission to bottom of South China Sea
  • Malaysia 1MDB scandal: Ex-PM Najib Razak’s wife Rosmah Mansor gives up claim on diamond bracelet seized in 2018 raid
  • Malaysia’s tourism sector stretched thin amid deluge of post-Covid travellers from Singapore, Indonesia, India and Europe
  • Japanese man arrested in Indonesia over Covid relief fraud, estimated at US$7.3 million
  • Thai dissident attacker jailed in Japan for breaking into home and using tear gas
  • Philippine banana growers plead for Japanese consumers to bear price hikes – farmers on brink of bankruptcy
  • Thailand’s ‘Ferrari’ police officer gets life for brutal torture and death of suspect
  • Vietnam’s health minister arrested over price gouging of Covid-19 tests
  • Thai railway market beloved by durian-hunting Chinese tourists makes a post-Covid comeback – even if they haven’t
  • Bon Odori Festival: Malaysian minister tells Muslims to avoid ‘un-Islamic’ Japanese dance event
  • New Zealand plans to charge farmers for sheep and cow burps to curb methane emissions
  • Thailand decriminalises cannabis on Thursday. What’s going to change?
  • No peace for South Korea’s Moon Jae-in, or fellow retirees, as noisy, rude protesters bombard his now-not-so-sleepy village
  • South China Sea: Australia PM insists Chinese jet’s ‘very dangerous’ intercept of patrol plane was in ‘international airspace’
  • Is monkeypox a ‘side effect’ of AstraZeneca’s Covid-19 vaccine? Korean expert slams ‘very ignorant rumour’
  • Why Indonesians of different faiths are mourning Ahmad Syafi’i Ma’arif, the Islamic scholar who preached ‘co-existence’ over tolerance
  • US must accept Southeast Asia wants China to play a central Indo-Pacific role
  • India’s BJP asks officials to exercise caution on religious issues after Islamic nations protest
  • Russia’s Aeroflot plans US$3 billion cash injection, said to eye big jet order
  • No breakthrough after Russia’s Lavrov in Türkiye for talks on Ukraine grain exports
  • As US Capitol riot public hearings begin, who will be held accountable?
  • Brazil police open criminal probe amid search for British journalist Dom Phillips in Amazon jungle
  • Gold coins seen in unprecedented images of treasure-laden San Jose shipwreck off Colombia’s Caribbean coast
  • ‘Sauce-obsessed’ US man accused of killing Chinese food delivery worker over sweet-and-sour duck sauce
  • Amid Ukraine war, Angela Merkel defends her Russia legacy, says there’s ‘nothing to apologise for’
  • ‘Unburnable’ copy of Handmaid’s Tale auctioned for US$130,000
  • Brad Pitt says Angelina Jolie sought ‘harm’ by selling vineyard to Russian oligarch
  • Matthew McConaughey makes tearful plea for US gun laws after massacre in Texas hometown
  • Russia claims ‘97 per cent control’ of flashpoint Luhansk province in eastern Ukraine
  • Ex-US schoolteacher pleads guilty to leading Islamic State all-woman brigade
  • New ‘doggy bag’ law for bars and restaurants in Spain set to be enforced in order to limit waste
  • Why it is in US interests to give up its dollar privilege to forge a more neutral financial order
  • Baidu’s Jidu Auto releases virtual concept robocar as intelligent EV unit moves towards mass production in 2023
  • Hong Kong IPO forecast slashed in half, as PwC expects 2022 proceeds to shrink 40 per cent amid tumbling valuations
  • A World Data Organisation needed to avoid rules-based disorder
  • Chinese tech stocks are either a good buy or a trap in market slump, investors say
  • British wealth manager SJP eyes China’s growing pension market, details retirement risk for Hong Kong’s ‘sandwiched generation’
  • Overseas traders return to Chinese onshore stocks, tucking into blue chips amid longest buying streak this year
  • Chip-making tools firm ASML to hire 200 staff in China as Covid restrictions ease, including sanctions compliance role
  • Elon Musk’s Twitter deal draws lawsuit from investor with five shares seeking info about bots
  • Alibaba’s 10 per cent surge fans rally in Hong Kong stocks on bets new gaming approvals signal end of China crackdown
  • Mainland Chinese buyers expected to flock to luxury Hong Kong housing now that Shanghai’s lockdown is over, developer says
  • Why US will fail to scuttle China’s economic integration with the rest of Asia
  • Hong Kong proptech start-up Butler expands beyond concierge services, signs up Swire, New World and Sun Hung Kai, eyes Singapore
  • Five key real estate priorities for Asia Pacific companies, and what this means for the post-pandemic office of the future
  • Shenzhen wants to double semiconductor output by 2025 under new plan to boost tech industry
  • How reading Stephen Covey’s The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People changed the life of NGO Light Be founder Ricky Yu
  • In K-drama Why Her? Seo Hyun-jin plays a vicious lawyer who falls from grace. Will she recover – and show us some human foibles?
  • Hong Kong art exhibition of Francis Bacon, George Condo and other artists’ portraits asks: what does it mean when one’s face ceases to be recognisable?
  • Unveiling new Dior chair, Philippe Starck predicts end of design: ‘Look at your iPhone – the number of products it replaces, it’s extraordinary’
  • Diabetes drug could be weight-loss miracle: patients shed up to 21 per cent of their body weight in trial of tirzepatide
  • Cherry Magic! movie review: Japanese boys’ love drama forgets the humour of its TV original, and is an ordeal to watch
  • How accessible design turned a Hong Kong apartment into a safe, wheelchair-friendly home for elderly couple to ‘comfortably grow a little older’
  • The best seafood by the ocean and street-style dining in Hong Kong, plus Japanese hidden gems: a former fast food promoter’s picks
  • What to see at the newly opened M+ Cinema in Hong Kong’s museum of visual culture
  • K-pop in 2022 so far: Le Sserafim chaos, WJSN’s Queendom 2 win confusion, concert tours missing stars – it’s been an up and down year
  • Gwen Stefani’s make-up line GXVE Beauty, four years in the making, is all her
  • Who’s the daddy? Celeb kids with secret parents, from Amber Heard’s baby Oonagh Paige and Geri Horner’s firstborn Bluebell, to Mindy Kaling’s two children and Cristiano Ronaldo’s son Jr.
  • Why rare gems are breaking records at auction in Hong Kong: Christie’s and Sotheby’s natural coloured jewels are raking in millions, including a vivid blue diamond that fetched US$57.47 million
  • The 12 best French restaurants in Hong Kong, from three-Michelin-starred Caprice at Four Seasons Hong Kong to the city’s first fine dining restaurant, Gaddi’s
  • Who is Stranger Things 4’s Joseph Quinn? Playing season newbie Eddie Munson, the 29-year-old British actor also appeared in Game of Thrones and hopes to star in Lord of the Rings
  • Move over, Scott Disick! Meet Sofia Richie’s new fiancé, Elliot Grainge: his dad is CEO of Universal Music Group and he founded his own label, 10K Projects – but do Lionel and Nicole approve?
  • The most expensive Kardashian-Jenner properties, ranked: from Kim, Kris, Khloé and Kylie’s Hidden Hills homes, to multimillion-dollar mansions shared with exes Kanye West and Travis Scott
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

  • ‘Yumi’s Cells’ makes confident return

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
